name: Neon Tetra
description: One of the most popular aquarium fish. Stunning iridescent blue and red coloring. Must be kept in schools.
care_tips: Keep in groups of 6+, planted tank preferred, peaceful community fish.

name: Betta Fish
description: Stunning flowing fins and vibrant colors. Males must be kept alone or with peaceful tankmates.
care_tips: One male per tank, needs surface access to breathe, warm water essential.

name: Guppy
description: Colorful and active livebearers. Breed readily in home aquariums. Many color varieties.
care_tips: Keep more females than males, prolific breeders, easy for beginners.

name: Corydoras Catfish
description: Adorable armored bottom-dwellers. Excellent tank cleaners. Very social fish.
care_tips: Keep in groups of 6+, sand substrate preferred, peaceful community fish.

name: Angelfish
description: Elegant cichlids with tall, triangular bodies. A centerpiece fish for larger tanks.
care_tips: Tall tank needed, may eat small fish, can be territorial when breeding.

name: Platy
description: Hardy and colorful livebearers. Many color varieties available. Great for beginners.
care_tips: Easy to breed, keep more females than males, very hardy.

name: Molly
description: Larger livebearers available in many colors including black, silver, and dalmatian.
care_tips: Prefer harder water, can tolerate brackish, prolific breeders.

name: Cherry Barb
description: Peaceful barb with beautiful red coloring in males. Less nippy than other barbs.
care_tips: Keep in groups, males color up in planted tanks, peaceful community fish.

name: Zebra Danio
description: Hardy active fish with horizontal stripes. Great for beginners and cycling tanks.
care_tips: Very active swimmers, need swimming space, tolerates cooler water.

name: Dwarf Gourami
description: Beautiful labyrinth fish with vibrant colors. Males are more colorful than females.
care_tips: One male per tank, surface access needed, can be shy.

name: Bristlenose Pleco
description: Excellent algae eater that stays small. Males develop bristles on their nose.
care_tips: Needs driftwood, supplement with vegetables, nocturnal.

name: Cardinal Tetra
description: Similar to neon tetra but with fuller red stripe. Stunning in large schools.
care_tips: Soft acidic water preferred, keep in large groups, sensitive to water quality.

name: Harlequin Rasbora
description: Beautiful coppery fish with distinctive black wedge marking. Very peaceful.
care_tips: Keep in groups of 8+, planted tanks ideal, peaceful community fish.

name: German Blue Ram
description: Stunning dwarf cichlid with iridescent blue and gold coloring. Peaceful for a cichlid.
care_tips: Needs warm water, soft acidic conditions, sensitive to water quality.

name: Kuhli Loach
description: Eel-like loach with orange and brown bands. Shy and nocturnal but fascinating.
care_tips: Keep in groups, sand substrate essential, provide hiding spots.

name: Black Skirt Tetra
description: Hardy tetra with distinctive black flowing fins. Can be slightly nippy with slow tankmates.
care_tips: Keep in groups of 6+, may nip fins of slow fish, very hardy.

name: Rummy Nose Tetra
description: Elegant tetra with bright red nose and striped tail. Red nose indicates health.
care_tips: Excellent water quality required, keep in large schools, nose fades when stressed.

name: Emperor Tetra
description: Regal tetra with purple iridescence and distinctive forked tail. Males have extended rays.
care_tips: Planted tanks preferred, keep in groups, peaceful community fish.

name: Congo Tetra
description: Large stunning African tetra with iridescent rainbow colors. Males have flowing fins.
care_tips: Needs swimming space, soft lighting enhances colors, keep in groups.

name: Serpae Tetra
description: Vibrant red tetra that can be nippy. Best kept in larger groups to reduce aggression.
care_tips: Keep in groups of 8+, avoid slow/long-finned tankmates, can be fin nippers.

name: Glowlight Tetra
description: Peaceful tetra with glowing orange-red stripe. Beautiful in planted tanks.
care_tips: Keep in groups, planted tanks enhance colors, very peaceful.

name: Diamond Tetra
description: Sparkling scales that shimmer like diamonds. Adults are truly stunning.
care_tips: Colors improve with age, needs good water quality, peaceful.

name: Flame Tetra
description: Hardy tetra with fiery red-orange coloration. Great for beginners.
care_tips: Very hardy, keep in groups, colors intensify in planted tanks.

name: Black Phantom Tetra
description: Elegant smoky grey tetra with black markings. Males display to each other harmlessly.
care_tips: Keep in groups, males will display but rarely fight, peaceful.

name: Lemon Tetra
description: Subtle yellow tetra with red eye. Colors intensify in mature, well-maintained tanks.
care_tips: Colors develop over time, soft acidic water preferred, peaceful.

name: Bleeding Heart Tetra
description: Beautiful larger tetra with red heart-shaped spot. Males have extended fins.
care_tips: Soft acidic water preferred, keep in groups, needs swimming space.

name: Buenos Aires Tetra
description: Hardy tetra that will eat plants. Very adaptable but can be nippy.
care_tips: Will destroy most live plants, keep in groups, very hardy.

name: Silvertip Tetra
description: Active copper-colored tetra with silvery fin tips. Very energetic schooler.
care_tips: Very active, keep in groups, may outcompete slow feeders.

name: Red Eye Tetra
description: Hardy tetra with distinctive red eye rim and black tail spot.
care_tips: Very hardy, adaptable to various conditions, keep in groups.

name: X-Ray Tetra
description: Translucent body reveals internal skeleton. Hardy and adaptable.
care_tips: Very hardy, can tolerate brackish water, peaceful schooler.

name: Penguin Tetra
description: Distinctive swimmer that swims at an upward angle. Black stripe runs along body.
care_tips: Active swimmer, keep in groups, unique swimming posture is normal.

name: Swordtail
description: Males have distinctive sword-like tail extension. Many color varieties.
care_tips: Keep more females than males, may jump, prefers harder water.

name: Endler's Livebearer
description: Smaller cousin of guppies with brilliant neon colors. Males are very colorful.
care_tips: Prolific breeders, keep more females, will hybridize with guppies.

name: Sailfin Molly
description: Large molly with impressive dorsal fin in males. Needs pristine water.
care_tips: Prefers hard alkaline water, can go brackish, needs space.

name: Balloon Molly
description: Round-bodied molly variant. Slower swimmer due to body shape.
care_tips: Cannot compete with fast fish, hard water preferred, shorter lifespan.

name: Otocinclus
description: Tiny algae eaters that are great for planted tanks. Very gentle.
care_tips: Keep in groups of 6+, need algae/biofilm, sensitive to water quality.

name: Pictus Catfish
description: Attractive spotted catfish with long barbels. Active swimmer.
care_tips: Will eat small fish, keep in groups, needs swimming space.

name: Glass Catfish
description: Completely transparent body showing internal organs. Unique and delicate.
care_tips: Keep in groups of 6+, needs pristine water, can be shy.

name: Upside-Down Catfish
description: Swims upside down to feed from surface. Fascinating behavior.
care_tips: Provide hiding spots, often seen at night, keep in groups.

name: Panda Cory
description: Adorable cory with panda-like markings. Prefers slightly cooler water.
care_tips: Keep in groups of 6+, sand substrate, cooler than most tropicals.

name: Bronze Cory
description: Very hardy and common cory. Bronze-green coloration. Great for beginners.
care_tips: Very hardy, keep in groups, sand substrate preferred.

name: Peppered Cory
description: Hardy cory that tolerates cooler water. Mottled grey-green coloration.
care_tips: Tolerates cooler water, keep in groups, very hardy.

name: Sterbai Cory
description: Beautiful spotted cory that tolerates warmer water. Great with discus.
care_tips: Can handle warmer water than most corys, keep in groups.

name: Pygmy Cory
description: Tiny cory that swims mid-water more than other species. Adorable in groups.
care_tips: Keep in large groups of 10+, may be eaten by larger fish.

name: Julii Cory
description: Attractive spotted cory often confused with C. trilineatus. Fine leopard spots.
care_tips: Keep in groups of 6+, sand substrate, most sold are actually trilineatus.

name: Discus
description: King of the aquarium. Stunning disc-shaped cichlids requiring expert care.
care_tips: Very warm water required, daily water changes for best health, keep in groups.

name: Cockatoo Dwarf Cichlid
description: Colorful dwarf cichlid with distinctive dorsal fin. Males are stunning.
care_tips: One male with multiple females, caves for breeding, soft water preferred.

name: Borelli's Dwarf Cichlid
description: One of the most peaceful Apistogrammas. Beautiful blue coloration.
care_tips: Tolerates cooler water, caves needed, peaceful for a cichlid.

name: Kribensis
description: Beautiful West African dwarf cichlid. Females develop bright pink belly.
care_tips: Pairs will breed readily, caves required, can be territorial when breeding.

name: Oscar
description: Intelligent and personable but large and messy. Will eat anything that fits.
care_tips: Needs very large tank, powerful filtration, messy eater.

name: Convict Cichlid
description: Hardy striped cichlid that breeds prolifically. Very aggressive when breeding.
care_tips: Will take over tank when breeding, very hardy, not for community tanks.

name: Jack Dempsey
description: Beautiful iridescent spots but aggressive. Electric blue variety is popular.
care_tips: Needs space, will rearrange tank, can be aggressive to tankmates.

name: Electric Blue Acara
description: Stunning electric blue coloration. One of the more peaceful medium cichlids.
care_tips: Relatively peaceful for a cichlid, may dig, beautiful specimen fish.

name: Firemouth Cichlid
description: Beautiful cichlid with fiery red throat. Displays by flaring gills.
care_tips: More bark than bite, displays but usually doesn't fight, needs caves.

name: Severum
description: Peaceful for a large cichlid. Often called poor man's discus.
care_tips: Relatively peaceful, may eat plants, multiple color varieties.

name: Yellow Lab Cichlid
description: One of the most peaceful Mbuna. Bright yellow coloration.
care_tips: Hard alkaline water required, keep with other Mbuna, overstocking reduces aggression.

name: Peacock Cichlid
description: Stunning African cichlids with incredible coloration. Males are magnificent.
care_tips: Males more colorful, hard alkaline water required, multiple varieties.

name: Jewel Cichlid
description: Brilliant red coloration intensifies during breeding. Very aggressive.
care_tips: Extremely aggressive when breeding, stunning coloration, not for community.

name: Tiger Barb
description: Active striped barb known for fin nipping. Keep in large groups to curb aggression.
care_tips: Keep 8+ to reduce nipping, avoid long-finned tankmates, very active.

name: Rosy Barb
description: Hardy subtropical barb. Males turn rosy pink during breeding.
care_tips: Tolerates cooler water, keep in groups, may nip slow fish.

name: Gold Barb
description: Hardy golden barb suitable for cooler tanks. Less nippy than tiger barbs.
care_tips: Tolerates cooler water, peaceful compared to other barbs, keep in groups.

name: Denison Barb
description: Stunning torpedo-shaped barb with red stripe. Active swimmer needing space.
care_tips: Needs excellent water quality and flow, keep in groups, active swimmers.

name: Odessa Barb
description: Males develop brilliant red band during spawning. Peaceful for a barb.
care_tips: Keep in groups, males color up beautifully, peaceful.

name: Checker Barb
description: Peaceful barb with checkerboard pattern. Great community fish.
care_tips: Very peaceful for a barb, keep in groups, good for planted tanks.

name: Chili Rasbora
description: Tiny brilliant red micro fish. Perfect for nano tanks.
care_tips: Keep in large groups of 10+, soft acidic water, nano tank stars.

name: Celestial Pearl Danio
description: Galaxy Rasbora. Stunning spotted pattern with red fins. Nano tank favorite.
care_tips: Males display to each other, planted tanks essential, avoid large tankmates.

name: Emerald Eye Rasbora
description: Elegant rasbora with glowing green eyes. Peaceful schooler.
care_tips: Keep in groups, soft water preferred, peaceful community fish.

name: Scissortail Rasbora
description: Large active rasbora with distinctive scissor-like tail movement.
care_tips: Needs swimming space, very active, keep in groups.

name: Lambchop Rasbora
description: Similar to harlequin but with smaller lambchop-shaped marking. Copper colored.
care_tips: Keep in groups of 8+, planted tanks ideal, very peaceful.

name: Phoenix Rasbora
description: Tiny red micro rasbora. Perfect for nano planted tanks.
care_tips: Very small, keep with similar sized fish, soft acidic water.

name: Clown Loach
description: Iconic orange and black striped loach. Gets very large and lives long.
care_tips: Grows slowly but large, keep in groups of 5+, needs hiding spots.

name: Yoyo Loach
description: Active loach with Y-O pattern on body. Great snail controller.
care_tips: Will eat snails, keep in groups, provide hiding spots.

name: Hillstream Loach
description: Flat-bodied loach adapted for fast-flowing water. Needs high oxygen.
care_tips: Needs cool water and high flow, grazes on biofilm, keep in groups.

name: Dojo Loach
description: Weather loach. Active before storms. Very personable and hardy.
care_tips: Tolerates cold water, very hardy, may burrow in substrate.

name: Zebra Loach
description: Attractive striped loach smaller than clown loach. Excellent snail control.
care_tips: Keep in groups of 5+, eats snails, provide hiding spots.

name: Dwarf Chain Loach
description: Tiny active loach with chain-link pattern. More active than most loaches.
care_tips: Keep in groups of 6+, very active during day, small snail control.

name: Pearl Gourami
description: Stunning pearlescent spots. One of the most beautiful and peaceful gouramis.
care_tips: Surface access needed, one male per tank, peaceful centerpiece.

name: Honey Gourami
description: Small peaceful gourami with honey-gold coloration. Males turn orange when breeding.
care_tips: Very peaceful, can be shy, surface access needed.

name: Moonlight Gourami
description: Silvery gourami with ethereal moonlight sheen. Peaceful and elegant.
care_tips: Likes warm water, surface access needed, peaceful despite size.

name: Blue Gourami
description: Hardy and common gourami. Also called Three-spot Gourami. Can be territorial.
care_tips: One per tank usually, hardy but can be aggressive, surface access needed.

name: Kissing Gourami
description: Famous for kissing behavior which is actually sparring. Gets large.
care_tips: Kissing is aggression not affection, gets big, needs space.

name: Sparkling Gourami
description: Tiny gourami that makes croaking sounds. Iridescent blue spots.
care_tips: Makes audible croaking, surface access needed, nano tank suitable.

name: Paradise Fish
description: Hardy colorful labyrinth fish tolerating cool water. Very aggressive.
care_tips: Very aggressive, tolerates unheated tanks, one male per tank.

name: Cherry Shrimp
description: Popular dwarf shrimp ranging from pale to deep red. Excellent algae eaters.
care_tips: Many fish will eat them, breed readily, easy for beginners.

name: Amano Shrimp
description: Excellent algae eater named after famous aquascaper Takashi Amano.
care_tips: Best algae eating shrimp, won't breed in freshwater, keep in groups.

name: Ghost Shrimp
description: Nearly transparent shrimp. Inexpensive and hardy. Good scavengers.
care_tips: Often sold as feeders but make good tank cleaners, short lifespan.

name: Crystal Red Shrimp
description: Stunning red and white bee shrimp. Requires specific water parameters.
care_tips: Soft acidic water required, sensitive to parameter swings, expert level.

name: Blue Velvet Shrimp
description: Vibrant blue color variety of cherry shrimp. Same easy care.
care_tips: Don't mix with other neocaridina colors, breed readily, easy care.

name: Bamboo Shrimp
description: Filter feeding shrimp that fans water for particles. Fascinating behavior.
care_tips: Needs current to filter feed, target feed if needed, peaceful.

name: Nerite Snail
description: Excellent algae eaters that won't reproduce in freshwater. Many patterns.
care_tips: Can't breed in freshwater, may lay eggs on decor, best algae eaters.

name: Mystery Snail
description: Large colorful snails. Lay eggs above waterline. Many color varieties.
care_tips: Lay pink egg clutches above water, need calcium, don't eat plants.

name: Ramshorn Snail
description: Flat-coiled snails that breed prolifically. Good cleanup crew.
care_tips: Breed rapidly, control by limiting food, come in many colors.

name: Assassin Snail
description: Predatory snail that eats pest snails. Yellow and brown striped.
care_tips: Will eat other snails, slower breeding than pests, attractive.

name: Malaysian Trumpet Snail
description: Burrowing snail that aerates substrate. Breeds prolifically.
care_tips: Excellent substrate aerators, nocturnal, can overpopulate.

name: Rabbit Snail
description: Large elegant snails with rabbit-like face. Slow breeders.
care_tips: Live bearers, slow reproducers, need warm alkaline water.

name: Clown Killifish
description: Tiny colorful killifish perfect for nano tanks. Rocket-shaped tail.
care_tips: Surface dweller, tight-fitting lid needed, likes floating plants.

name: Golden Wonder Killifish
description: Predatory surface dweller. Metallic gold coloration. Will eat small fish.
care_tips: Will eat anything that fits in mouth, surface predator, lid required.

name: Bluefin Notho
description: Stunning annual killifish. Males are incredibly colorful. Short lifespan.
care_tips: Annual species with short lifespan, can be bred with peat storage.

name: Least Killifish
description: One of smallest livebearer fish. Native to southeastern US.
care_tips: Actually a livebearer not killifish, breeds readily, nano tank.

name: Boesemani Rainbowfish
description: Stunning two-toned rainbow fish. Blue front half, orange-yellow rear.
care_tips: Colors develop with age, keep in groups, needs swimming space.

name: Neon Dwarf Rainbowfish
description: Small rainbow with neon blue body and red fins. Active schooler.
care_tips: Great for smaller tanks, active swimmers, keep in groups.

name: Turquoise Rainbowfish
description: Beautiful turquoise-blue coloration. From Lake Kutubu in Papua New Guinea.
care_tips: Prefers slightly cooler water, keep in groups, peaceful.

name: Madagascar Rainbowfish
description: Elongated rainbow from Madagascar. Yellow body with black stripe.
care_tips: Needs excellent water quality, keep in groups, active swimmer.

name: Red Irian Rainbowfish
description: Large rainbow where males turn brilliant red. Impressive display fish.
care_tips: Males turn red with age, females silver, keep in groups.

name: Celebes Rainbowfish
description: Delicate rainbow with extended fins. Males have long fin rays.
care_tips: Prefers hard alkaline water, extended fins may be nipped, keep in groups.

name: Forktail Blue-Eye Rainbowfish
description: Tiny rainbow with forked tail and blue eyes. Males display beautifully.
care_tips: Keep in groups, males display constantly, peaceful nano rainbow.

name: Spotted Blue-Eye Rainbowfish
description: Tiny spotted rainbow with bright blue eyes. Males have extended fins.
care_tips: Nano tank suitable, keep in groups, males display to females.

name: White Cloud Mountain Minnow
description: Hardy coldwater fish. Colorful and active. Great for unheated tanks.
care_tips: Prefers cooler water, very hardy, keep in groups.

name: Leopard Danio
description: Spotted variant of zebra danio. Same hardy care requirements.
care_tips: Very hardy, active swimmers, tolerates cooler water.

name: Pearl Danio
description: Pearlescent danio with iridescent sheen. Active schooler.
care_tips: Hardy and active, tolerates cooler water, keep in groups.

name: Giant Danio
description: Large active danio. May intimidate smaller fish with activity.
care_tips: Very active swimmers, need space, may outcompete slow feeders.

name: Longfin Zebra Danio
description: Long-finned variety of zebra danio. Flowing fins require gentle tankmates.
care_tips: Avoid fin nippers, hardy otherwise, keep in groups.

name: Gold Ring Danio
description: Tiny danio with spotted pattern. Perfect for smaller tanks.
care_tips: Keep in larger groups, active little fish, peaceful.

name: Siamese Algae Eater
description: Excellent algae eater including black beard algae. Active and hardy.
care_tips: True SAE eats BBA, may become territorial with age, keep in groups.

name: Flying Fox
description: Often confused with SAE. More territorial and less effective at algae.
care_tips: Territorial with similar fish, often sold as SAE, one per tank.

name: Red Tail Shark
description: Striking black body with red tail. Very territorial as adults.
care_tips: One per tank, becomes more aggressive with age, needs hiding spots.

name: Rainbow Shark
description: Grey body with red-orange fins. Territorial but less so than red-tail.
care_tips: One per tank, territorial to similar fish, needs space.

name: Common Pleco
description: Gets very large despite small sale size. Not suitable for most tanks.
care_tips: Grows massive, needs very large tank, often stunted in small tanks.

name: Rubber Lip Pleco
description: Stays reasonably small. Good alternative to common pleco.
care_tips: Prefers cooler water with current, good algae eater, needs driftwood.

name: Clown Pleco
description: Small striped pleco. Primarily eats wood rather than algae.
care_tips: Requires driftwood, doesn't eat much algae, good for small tanks.

name: Royal Pleco
description: Large beautiful striped pleco. Heavy wood eater.
care_tips: Gets very large, must have driftwood, produces lots of waste.

name: Bala Shark
description: Not a shark but a large cyprinid. Often bought small, gets massive.
care_tips: Gets very large, needs huge tank, keep in groups of 5+.

name: Tinfoil Barb
description: Large schooling barb. Will eat plants. Needs huge tank.
care_tips: Gets massive, destroys plants, needs very large tank.
